t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 1 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 10.12 and iOS 10.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 10 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 11 and iOS 14.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 11 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 11.5 and iOS 14.5.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 12 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 12 and iOS 15.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 13 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 12.3 and iOS 15.4.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 14 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 13 and iOS 16.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 2 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 10.12.1 and iOS 10.1.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 3 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 10.13 and iOS 11.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 4 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 10.14.1 and iOS 12.1.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 5 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 10.14.2 and iOS 12.1.1.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 6 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 10.14.4 and iOS 12.2.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 7 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 10.14.6 and iOS 12.4.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 8 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 10.15.1 and iOS 13.2.

t: Apple Pay on the Web Version 9 Release Notes
a: The version of Apple Pay available in macOS 10.15.6 and iOS 13.6.

t: Apple Pay on the Web version history
a: Learn about features in each version of Apple Pay on the Web.
A dictionary that represents a request to set up an automatic reload payment, such as a store card top-up or a prepaid account.
An object that displays a button either to trigger payments through Apple Pay or to prompt the user to set up a card.
A type that indicates the languages and regions that you can specify for the Apple Pay button.
A type that indicates the available appearances for an Apple Pay Button.
A type that indicates the button types that you can display to initiate Apple Pay transactions.
Field names for requesting contact information in a payment request.
An event object that contains the coupon code entered by the user.
A dictionary that contains the updated coupon code.
A dictionary that contains the updated transaction details for responding to a coupon changed event.
A dictionary that specifies the values for the calendrical units for a date.
A dictionary that specifies the start and end dates for a range of time.
A dictionary that represents a request to set up a deferred payment, such as a hotel booking or a pre-order.
A customizable error type that you create to indicate problems with the address or contact information on an Apple Pay sheet.
Creates an Apple Pay error object.
The error code that indicates whether an error on the payment sheet is for shipping or billing information, or for another kind of error.
Names of the fields in the shipping or billing contact information, used to locate errors in the payment sheet.
Values you use to enable or disable Apple Pay Later for a specific transaction.
A line item in a payment request—for example, total, tax, discount, or grand total.
A type that indicates whether a line item is final or pending.
The payment capabilities the merchant supports.
A dictionary that defines the Apple Pay modifiers for a payment type in the W3C Payment Request API.
The result of authorizing a payment request that contains payment information.
The result of payment authorization, including status and errors.
An event object that contains the token used to authorize a payment.
Contact information fields to use for billing and shipping contact information.
A dictionary that describes an Apple Pay payment method.
An event object that contains the payment method.
A string that represents the type of the payment method.
Updated transaction details to provide after the user changes the payment method in the payment sheet.
A dictionary that contains metadata related to an order.
A provisioned payment card for Apple Pay payments.
Payment pass activation states.
A request for payment, which includes information about payment-processing capabilities, the payment amount, and shipping information.
A type that indicates the time a payment occurs in a transaction.
An object that contains the user’s payment credentials.
A dictionary that defines the context for a single payment token in a payment request for multimerchant payments.
A type that indicates calendrical units, such as year, month, day, and hour.
A dictionary that represents a request to set up a recurring payment, typically a subscription.
A dictionary that defines the Apple Pay payment request object to use for the W3C Payment Request API.
A dictionary that defines basic payment and contact information that the Apple Pay payment request object uses for the W3C Payment Request API.
The entry point for Apple Pay on the web.
A session object for managing the payment process on the web.
Values that indicate whether the shipping mode prevents the user from editing fields of the shipping address.
An event object that contains the shipping address the user selects.
Updated transaction details that result from a change in shipping contact, including any errors.
A dictionary that describes the shipping method for delivering physical goods.
An event object that contains the shipping method.
Updated transaction details that result from a change in shipping method.
A type that indicates how to ship purchased items.
An event object that contains the validation URL.
Use the Apple Pay JS API to check whether Apple Pay is available, to check whether a device has a payment credential provisioned in Wallet, and to determine when to display an Apple Pay button.
